L1.area: Token signature verification and validation logic

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the token verification logic unconditionally accepts tokens that specify no signing algorithm, immediately returning a successful verification result without performing any cryptographic checks. An attacker can craft tokens with arbitrary claims that bypass all verification, enabling complete impersonation and privilege escalation. The vulnerability requires no knowledge of the signing key and works against all signature algorithm configurations.

L3.area: JWS signature verification and JWT claim validator pipeline for signing algorithm enforcement
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists where the signature verification logic unconditionally accepts tokens that specify the 'none' signing algorithm, immediately returning a successful verification result without any cryptographic checks or requiring explicit developer opt-in. Additionally, the JWT-level verification API does not enforce a signing algorithm restriction by default, allowing forged tokens with no cryptographic protection to pass through the complete verification pipeline. An attacker can forge tokens with arbitrary claims, enabling impersonation and privilege escalation without knowledge of any signing key.

ground_truth.reason: The JWS verify function returns true immediately when the token header specifies algorithm 'none', without performing any cryptographic verification or requiring explicit developer opt-in, allowing attackers to forge arbitrary valid tokens.
